description: LTX-2.3 22B image-to-video (and text-to-video) GENERATION — the natural home for stacking Tier-1 effect/style/camera LoRAs from the catalog. Graph: Lightricks/ComfyUI-LTXVideo example 'LTX-2.3_T2V_I2V_Single_Stage_Distilled_Full' (the cloud GemmaAPITextEncode nodes are bypassed; the local CLIPTextEncode + LTXAVTextEncoderLoader path is active, so no external API key is needed).

about.what: Creates a brand-new video — either starting from one image you provide, or from a written description alone. This is the main place to add 'look' extras (style, camera moves, effects) from the LoRA list.
about.inputs: A starting image (for image-to-video) and/or a written description of the scene and action. Optional style/effect add-ons.
about.key_knobs: Prompt — one flowing paragraph covering scene, action, camera move and lighting. Frames — how long the clip is. Resolution — the output size.

_TEMPLATE: This preset is now FILLED / ready-to-run: the TWO model URLs below (dev checkpoint + gemma encoder) are pinned to real HF URLs — the 'dev' checkpoint and the gemma text encoder (Lightricks/LTX-2.3 + Comfy-Org/ltx-2, verified May 2026). Still run --convert-only ONCE to validate the converted graph before a paid render. NOTE on the distill LoRA: this no longer uses Lightricks' official rank-384 distilled LoRA — it ships the cond-safe rank-72 variant (TenStrip ...ceil72_condsafe) because the rank-384 LoRA is high-capacity enough to dampen conditioning signals on I2V / reference-driven inputs, which is exactly what this preset feeds it. The cond-safe build zeroes the cross-attention / adaLN scale-shift / gate layers, so conditioning passes through; it's safe up to strength 1.0 on a generation pass (the graph keeps its tuned 0.5 / 0.2). See README 'Cond-safe distill LoRA'.
_HOWITRUNS: Same no-build path as cleanplate: a public ComfyUI base image runs comfy_run.py, which seeds ComfyUI, clones node_packs, downloads the weights, converts the UI graph to API via /object_info, applies patches, splices the --lora stack onto the checkpoint's MODEL output (lora_chain anchor 3940 -> affects both the CFGGuider and MultimodalGuider passes), and renders. Heavier cold start than cleanplate (the dev checkpoint is large).
_LORASTACK: All --lora entries chain onto the checkpoint (anchor 3940) BEFORE the built-in distillation LoRAs' consumers, so a stacked effect/style LoRA layers with the distill LoRAs across both sampler passes. Effect LoRAs are i2v/t2v, which is exactly what this graph does — e.g. `--lora transition:1.0 shot.png --prompt '...'`.

ui.title: LTX-2.3 Generate — image/text-to-video (stack effect LoRAs here)

primary_input.label: Init image (i2v)
primary_input.tooltip: Starting frame for image-to-video. Add catalog effect LoRAs with the LoRA picker below.

prompt.default: A marketplace vendor arranges colorful spices in metal bowls as two customers browse nearby. The camera slowly pans across the display while ambient chatter fills the air.

ui.label: Prompt
ui.tooltip: LTX-2.3 works best as ONE flowing present-tense paragraph covering: shot type, scene + lighting, action over time, character details, camera move, and audio. Longer prompts fill longer clips; understood terms include 'slow dolly in', 'golden hour', 'shallow depth of field'. LoRA trigger words are auto-appended. (src: LTX-2.3 prompt guide, ltx.io)

negative.default: pc game, console game, video game, cartoon, childish, ugly

ui.label: Negative prompt
ui.tooltip: Lightricks' shipped LTX-2 negative (from their official example workflow). The prompt guide also says to AVOID putting readable text/logos, emotional labels, and complex physics in the positive prompt.

ui.label: Width
ui.tooltip: LTX-2.3 goes up to 4K / UHD (3840x2160); pick from the Resolution dropdown above for known-good sizes (custom values safest divisible by 32). Native portrait is 1080x1920. 720p-1080p is the sweet spot on a single GPU; 4K is heavy and only for clips <=10s. (src: LTX-2.3 docs)

ui.label: Height
ui.tooltip: LTX-2.3 up to 4K / UHD; pick from the Resolution dropdown above for known-good sizes (custom values safest divisible by 32). For 9:16 use e.g. 1080x1920 (the model composes natively for vertical). (src: LTX-2.3 docs)

ui.label: Frames (length)
ui.tooltip: Frame count; LTX REQUIRES 8*n+1 (..., 121, 161). Output container runs ~30fps here, so 121 frames ~= 4s. LTX-2.3 max ~10s @50fps (4K) or ~20s @25fps (1080p). (src: LTX-2.3 docs)

ui.label: Sampler steps
ui.tooltip: Denoising steps (LTXVScheduler). 15 is the tuned baseline. More steps = cleaner detail/motion at higher cost; diminishing returns past ~25-30. CLI: --set 4966.inputs.steps <n>. (src: LTX-2.3 example workflow)
